Notice Of And Motion For Issuance Of A Request For Judicial Assistance (Letter Of Request) Under The Hague Convention On The Taking Of Evidence Abroad In Civil Or Commercial Matters
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Mark L. Pettinari, Tel. No. 1-415-233-3346 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Cathrine M. Lee of Musick Peeler, Tel. No. 1-415-281-2024 appearing for the Defendant;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff David Kane's motion for issuance of a request for judicial assistance (letter of request) under the Hague Convention on the taking of evidence abroad in civil or commercial matters is granted. Regardless of who bears the burden, Mr. Kane has shown good cause for the taking of the oral examinations of Nadia Ferriday and Keith McGuinn in the United Kingdom. Defendant Joel Poloney's concerns about the cost of the examinations can be dealt with by his participating in the examinations by video conference or similar technology. The parties are required to meet and confer to ensure that, if desired, Mr. Poloney's counsel is able to participate in the examinations by video conference or similar technology. Order signed and Revised Letter Request for Judicial Assistance Issued by the court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
